title: Derived projection authority contract bounded
objective: Bound every derived-projection state class -- flat files, generated directories, build inputs, runtime materializations, reports, exports, and caches -- as a disposable or preserved projection with one-way provenance from canonical state (PostgreSQL+RuVector rows, or the intended profile package for the generated runtime). Each class declares its canonical source identity, selected branch and version, deterministic materializer, checksum and metadata contract, permitted edit surface, cleanup rule, and a proof that the projection output can never become a competing writable owner. This is the class-by-class refinement of STORE-001's 'derived projections' assignment (PostgreSQL as source of truth, projected by envctl / CodeDB materialization, write authority read-only, sync PostgreSQL -> filesystem forward-only, recovery by re-projection).

supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv (rows 9,16,20,25 materialization/export/musl; row 3,17 caches)
task PGAUTH-001 (durable macro-state authority = PostgreSQL canonical rows this projects from)
task POSTGRES-008 (envctl database-to-directory materialization: deterministic, byte-identical, disposable)
task FOUNDATION-003 (source -> package -> profile -> generated runtime; archive-first shadow retirement; Yazelix input-output-frontdoor ownership contract)

projection_classes_bounded: flat files; generated directories; build inputs; runtime materializations; reports; exports; caches

work_items.surface: Flat files -- materialized source and config files (.rs, .toml, .nu) concatenated from canonical PostgreSQL raw_code blocks
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL codebase rows (canonical source); envctl is the read-only projection writer
work_items.canonical_source_identity: PostgreSQL codebase_table rows for the production branch (raw_code, module_path, language, status='production'); each block carries its SHA-256 content identity under the BlobStore parity contract
work_items.selected_branch_and_version: envctl queries the final production branch at its pinned version (Blueprint Materialization step 1); the branch/version id is recorded on the projection
work_items.deterministic_materializer: envctl concatenation (Blueprint Materialization step 3 / POSTGRES-008): raw_code blocks joined in stored order into a complete standard file
work_items.checksum_and_metadata_contract: exact-byte materialization under one SHA-256 identity (ADOPT-001 'Exact-byte materialization'); repeated projection of the same version is byte-identical (POSTGRES-008 gate)
work_items.permitted_edit_surface: read-only; a source change is applied to the canonical row via SQL UPDATE and re-projected -- the flat file is never hand-edited
work_items.cleanup_rule: disposable; delete freely, regenerate by re-projection from PostgreSQL (STORE-001 recovery = re-projection)
work_items.non_competing_owner_proof: envctl write authority is read-only-from-PostgreSQL (STORE-001 derived-projections class); no reverse-sync path exists; blocked_paths forbid reverse synchronization into canonical state and generated-runtime hand-edit

work_items.surface: Generated directories -- the reconstructed module_path directory tree envctl builds in memory and dumps
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL module_path metadata (canonical source); envctl is the read-only projection writer
work_items.canonical_source_identity: PostgreSQL module_path metadata for the selected production branch (Blueprint Materialization step 2)
work_items.selected_branch_and_version: same production branch/version selected in PGAUTH-004-A; directory structure is a function of that version's module_path rows
work_items.deterministic_materializer: envctl directory reconstruction (Blueprint step 2 / POSTGRES-008): module_path metadata -> in-memory directory map -> dumped tree
work_items.checksum_and_metadata_contract: paths, byte content, ordering, permissions, and module relationships are deterministically reconstructed; repeated projection is byte-identical (POSTGRES-008 gate)
work_items.permitted_edit_surface: read-only; structural changes are made to canonical module_path rows, then re-projected -- the generated tree is never hand-edited
work_items.cleanup_rule: disposable; regenerable in full by re-projection
work_items.non_competing_owner_proof: the directory is a memory-then-disk projection of canonical metadata with no write path back; STORE-001 sync_direction is PostgreSQL -> envctl materialize -> filesystem (forward-only, derived)

work_items.surface: Build inputs -- the materialized source tree handed to the Nix / Yazelix build environment for compilation
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL production rows + committed Nix/source pins (canonical inputs); envctl is the read-only projection writer
work_items.canonical_source_identity: the PGAUTH-004-A/B projection (which traces to PostgreSQL production rows) plus pinned build inputs (committed source pins, unchanged per STORE-001 external-secret/provenance class)
work_items.selected_branch_and_version: the same production branch/version, resolved by the envctl export/release command before compilation
work_items.deterministic_materializer: envctl dump into the Nix/Yazelix build environment (Blueprint Materialization step 4); the compilation-input closure
work_items.checksum_and_metadata_contract: build input is the checksummed materialized tree plus pinned inputs; identical version + identical pins -> identical build input
work_items.permitted_edit_surface: read-only and consumed by the compiler; the build never writes back into the source tree or canonical rows
work_items.cleanup_rule: disposable; discarded after the build and regenerable by re-projection
work_items.non_competing_owner_proof: build inputs are consume-only compiler feedstock; they have no synchronization edge into PostgreSQL, so they cannot become an authority (STORE-001 forward-only invariant)

work_items.surface: Runtime materializations -- generated runtime profile and the launched portable folder (LifeOS UI + Yazelix/Zellij/Nushell env) reached through the runtime frontdoor
work_items.owner: the intended profile package (canonical source resolved source -> package -> profile per FOUNDATION-003); Yazelix input-output-frontdoor projects it
work_items.decision: disposable derived projection (regenerable from the profile package)
work_items.canonical_source_identity: the intended profile package that the runtime frontdoor resolves to (FOUNDATION-003: 'every runtime frontdoor resolves to the intended profile package')
work_items.selected_branch_and_version: the released profile package version/pin selected at launch
work_items.deterministic_materializer: profile -> generated-runtime materialization (FOUNDATION-003) driven through the Yazelix input-output-frontdoor initialization sequence
work_items.checksum_and_metadata_contract: generated runtime state is reproducible from the profile package (FOUNDATION-003 gate: 'generated state is reproducible')
work_items.permitted_edit_surface: read-only generated runtime; blocked_paths forbid generated-runtime hand-edit and profile mutation -- changes are made to the profile source and re-materialized
work_items.cleanup_rule: disposable; regenerable from the profile package; superseded shadow owners are archived-first, never deleted (FOUNDATION-003 archive-first retirement)
work_items.non_competing_owner_proof: the generated runtime is a frontdoor projection of the profile package with no reverse write to the profile or to canonical state; FOUNDATION-003 rollback restores the prior generation, confirming the projection is disposable and non-authoritative

work_items.surface: Reports -- ATAS simulated future timelines (projected git diffs, resource burn, complexity spikes, failure nodes), db_health/status reports, and evidence/proof receipts
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL current codebase state + agent failure history (ATAS canonical input); observed run evidence for receipts
work_items.decision: transient simulations disposable; evidence/proof receipts preserved as non-authoritative audit records
work_items.canonical_source_identity: PostgreSQL codebase state and agent failure-rate history used as the ESN initial condition (Blueprint ATAS section); for receipts, the observed revision/state they attest to
work_items.selected_branch_and_version: the codebase state/branch used as the ESN initial condition, or the observed revision recorded on the receipt
work_items.deterministic_materializer: ATAS Echo-State-Network ensemble simulation (Blueprint: parallel ESN instances forecasting up to 100 steps) for timelines; receipt emission for evidence records
work_items.checksum_and_metadata_contract: each report records the canonical source-state identity it was derived from; simulations are forecasts (state diffs / resource burn / failure nodes), never commits
work_items.permitted_edit_surface: read-only; a winning ATAS timeline enters canonical state only by merging its COW branch into the main trunk -- a PostgreSQL operation -- never by editing the report
work_items.cleanup_rule: transient simulations disposable; proof receipts and audit reports preserved as non-authoritative evidence
work_items.non_competing_owner_proof: reports are forecasts/observations with no write path to canonical state; the commit mechanism is COW-branch merge inside PostgreSQL (Blueprint COW branching), so the report itself can never become a writable owner

work_items.surface: Exports -- release/export trees: envctl export output, musl static binaries, and the portable 'install-in-place' folder / AppImage
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL production branch (via the materialized build input) + pinned Nix/musl inputs (canonical inputs); envctl export + Nix are the read-only projection writers
work_items.decision: preserved projection (shipped release retained) but non-authoritative and regenerable; intermediate /nix/store closure disposable
work_items.canonical_source_identity: the PGAUTH-004-C build input (tracing to PostgreSQL production rows) plus pinned Nix/musl inputs
work_items.selected_branch_and_version: the release branch/version selected by the envctl export/release command (Blueprint: 'issue an export or release command using envctl')
work_items.deterministic_materializer: envctl export -> Nix static musl compilation against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl (Blueprint Portability section; Context Matrix rows 9,20)
work_items.checksum_and_metadata_contract: the release artifact carries a content checksum and is regenerable from the same PostgreSQL version + pinned inputs; static musl bundling eliminates /nix/store RPATHs so the artifact is self-contained
work_items.permitted_edit_surface: read-only immutable artifact; a correction requires a new canonical version and a fresh re-export -- artifacts are never patched in place
work_items.cleanup_rule: shipped release preserved (retained, non-authoritative); the intermediate /nix/store build closure is disposable
work_items.non_competing_owner_proof: an exported artifact is a terminal read projection with no synchronization edge back into PostgreSQL; provenance is strictly one-way (STORE-001 forward-only), so it cannot become a competing owner

work_items.surface: Caches -- derived read-through caches: the redb BLAKE3 dedup / semantic / embedding cache (content-hash -> vector)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical semantic_embedding is the authority; redb owns only the transient cache STORAGE (STORE-001-B / PGAUTH-002), not the value's authority
work_items.decision: disposable derived projection (read-through cache of canonical embeddings)
work_items.canonical_source_identity: the canonical semantic_embedding stored in PostgreSQL+RuVector; the cache key is the BLAKE3 hash of the source text block (Blueprint dedup cache logic)
work_items.selected_branch_and_version: cache entries are keyed by content hash, not by branch; the authoritative vector version always lives in PostgreSQL
work_items.deterministic_materializer: cache miss -> all-MiniLM-L6-v2 re-embedding inside the bare-metal Rust binary (Blueprint ingestion; Context Matrix row 17); reproducible for identical content
work_items.checksum_and_metadata_contract: BLAKE3 content-hash identity (Blueprint); a cache value equals the canonical embedding computed for that exact content block
work_items.permitted_edit_surface: read-only cache; a vector is never authored independently of a canonical embedding -- envctl computes the embedding during the bridge and commits it to PostgreSQL
work_items.cleanup_rule: disposable; on cache loss the vector is deterministically re-embedded (STORE-001 semantic-class recovery: reproducible re-embedding)
work_items.non_competing_owner_proof: the cached vector is never the authority -- the PostgreSQL embedding is; storage ownership is deferred to redb's transient tier without reclassification (STORE-001 invariant: 'redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class'); the cache is a forward-only derivation

invariants: One-way provenance: every projection derives forward from canonical state (PostgreSQL+RuVector rows, or the intended profile package for the generated runtime); there is no reverse synchronization into canonical state.
Read-only projection writer: envctl (and Nix and ATAS) write projections but read only from canonical sources; the projection is never itself a writable owner.

Deterministic and reproducible: repeated materialization of one selected version is byte-identical (POSTGRES-008); disposable projections recover by re-projection, preserved projections stay non-authoritative and regenerable.
Edits go upstream: any change to projected content is applied to the canonical row (SQL UPDATE) or the profile source and then re-projected -- generated output is never hand-edited.
No competing owner: each projection class carries an explicit non-competing-owner proof; STORE-001's single durable writer (PostgreSQL) and forward-only sync are preserved end to end.
completion_gate: Every projected state class declares its canonical source identity, selected branch and version, deterministic materializer, checksum and metadata contract, permitted edit surface, cleanup rule, and proof that projection output cannot become a competing writable owner.
rollback_plan: Restore the prior task-graph revision; no projections, generated runtime, profiles, databases, or user-space sources change during planning.
